title: SMACH YAML to Mermaid flowchart transpiler
purpose: Converts SMACH state machine YAML definitions into Mermaid flowchart syntax for visualization. Used to transform hierarchical state machine configurations into graphical representations.
module_context: Exports two functions: transpile() for converting YAML to Mermaid, and isSmachYaml() for detecting SMACH YAML format. Defines STATE_SHAPES mapping for state type to Mermaid node shapes and helper functions for node ID generation and declaration.

steps.content: Sanitize the input name by replacing non-alphanumeric characters with underscores.
If a prefix is provided, concatenate it with the sanitized name using an underscore.
Return the resulting node ID string.

methods.name: nodeDeclaration
methods.normalized_params: (id: string, label: string, type: string): string

steps.content: Look up the shape for the given state type in the STATE_SHAPES mapping.
IF the shape is 'stadium', return a Mermaid stadium node declaration.
ELSE IF the shape is 'hexagon', return a Mermaid hexagon node declaration.
ELSE IF the shape is 'diamond', return a Mermaid diamond node declaration.
ELSE IF the shape is 'parallelogram', return a Mermaid parallelogram node declaration.
ELSE IF the shape is 'trapezoid', return a Mermaid trapezoid node declaration.
ELSE return a Mermaid rounded rectangle node declaration.

methods.name: processStates
methods.normalized_params: (states: Record<string, any>, lines: string[], externalLinks: string[], prefix: string, initialState: string, indent: string, parentOutcomes: string[], parentOutcomePrefix: string): void

steps.content: Iterate through each state in the input states collection.
Generate a node ID for the current state using the provided prefix.
Determine the state type from the configuration or default to CallbackState.
IF the state type is a container type, render it as a Mermaid subgraph.
ELSE render the state as a leaf node with appropriate shape.
Process transitions for the current state and add appropriate links to the lines or external links arrays.
Parse the input YAML text into a JavaScript object.
Validate that the parsed object contains a smach_diagram root.
Extract the root StateMachine from the diagram.
Initialize the Mermaid flowchart lines array with the flowchart TD header.
Add a start node and connect it to the root machine's initial state.
Process the root machine's states recursively to build the flowchart.
Add outcome nodes for the root machine.
Append external links for container outcome connections.
Return the complete Mermaid flowchart as a string.
Test if the input text matches the SMACH YAML pattern using a regular expression.
Return true if the pattern is found, false otherwise.
